Hello, everyone!

I'm <PRESIDIO_ANONYMIZED_PERSON>, here for the physical therapy assistant program. Originally from China, I recently moved to the states at the beginning of this year with my family and dog. While I used to work as a preschool teacher, I've always had a desire to pursue a career as a PTA. I'm excited to start this new journey and can't wait to meet all of you!

how do i sound like i was a preschool teacher, but here I moved to the states I want to switch  and pursue a career as a PTA

Hello, everyone!

I'm <PRESIDIO_ANONYMIZED_PERSON>, and I recently moved to the States from China with my family and dog. Back in my home country, I worked as a preschool teacher. However, upon coming here, I have decided to switch gears and pursue a new career as a Physical Therapy Assistant (PTA). I'm excited about this change and can't wait to begin this new journey. I'm looking forward to meeting all of you!

check my essay correct my grammar and change to the approiate words: It  was a long summer vacation after high school before college, I though I should do something , so I signed up for hip hop dance class where I found my potential for dancing. It was to my surpise it wasn’t diffcult for me to do all the dance movement and I was naturally good at grasping all the movement. That’s where dance journey began, after 2 years of dancing, I got an opportunity joining the dance competetion in hongkong, I worked hard with my group, 5 hours practice minum daily, I still remember that day 8 people tried their best on the stage, eventually we really won the first prize it gave me a lot of confidence especially, after college I chose to study early childhood education, and chinese traditional dance class was a very important part of it. Of course I excelled at that class, and later on I was able to assist professor to instruct others on their movement, I enjoyed the process of demoing the correct posture seeing all the improvements my classmates had done which was my biggest accomplishment.  And since now I will be joining the physical therapy program at Pima, I hope after I have completed my PMI program, I hope to contiune to help my patients to improve their physical condition and achive their goal on physical therapy.

It was a long summer vacation after high school before college, and I thought I should do something different. That's when I decided to sign up for a hip hop dance class, where I discovered my talent and potential for dancing. Surprisingly, I found the dance movements to be not difficult at all, and I naturally excelled in grasping them. This marked the beginning of my dance journey. After two years of dancing, I received an opportunity to participate in a dance competition in Hong Kong. I worked tirelessly with my group, practicing for a minimum of five hours daily. I vividly remember the day when eight of us gave our best performance onstage, and eventually, we won the first prize. This achievement boosted my confidence, and it motivated me to pursue a career in early childhood education after college. Chinese traditional dance, which played a crucial role in my education, proved to be my strong suit. I even had the privilege of assisting the professor in instructing others on their movements. Seeing my classmates' improvements and witnessing their progress was my biggest accomplishment. Now that I will be joining the physical therapy program at Pima, I hope that after completing my education, I can continue helping my patients improve their physical conditions and achieve their goals in physical therapy.

pursue a new career as a Physical Therapy Assistant (PTA). I’m excited about this change and can’t wait to begin this new journey. I’m looking forward to meeting all of you!
check my essay correct my grammar and change to the approiate words: It was a long summer vacation after high school before college, I though I should do something , so I signed up for hip hop dance class where I found my potential for dancing. It was to my surpise it wasn’t diffcult for me to do all the dance movement and I was naturally good at grasping all the movement. That’s where dance journey began, after 2 years of dancing, I got an opportunity joining the dance competetion in hongkong, I worked hard with my group, 5 hours practice minum daily, I still remember that day 8 people tried their best on the stage, eventually we really won the first prize it gave me a lot of confidence especially, in college I chose to study early childhood education, and chinese traditional dance class was a very important part of it. Of course I excelled at that class, and later on I was able to assist professor to instruct others on their movement, I enjoyed the process of demoing the correct posture seeing all the improvements my classmates had done which was my biggest accomplishment. And since now I will be joining the physical therapy program at Pima, I hope after I have completed my PMI program, I hope to contiune to help my patients to improve their physical condition and achive their goal on physical therapy.

It was a long summer vacation after high school, just before college, when I felt the need to explore something new. That's when I decided to enroll in a hip hop dance class, and it was there that I discovered my true potential for dancing. To my surprise, I found the dance movements to be quite effortless, and I had a natural talent for grasping them. This marked the beginning of my dance journey. After two years of dancing, I had the opportunity to participate in a dance competition in Hong Kong. I dedicated myself to rigorous practice sessions with my group, practicing for a minimum of five hours daily. I still remember that day when the eight of us showcased our best performance on stage. Eventually, we won the first prize, and it filled me with immense confidence.

When I entered college, I decided to pursue a degree in early childhood education. Chinese traditional dance became an integral part of my studies. Flourishing in that class, I soon found myself assisting the professor in instructing others on their movements. Witnessing the progress and improvement of my classmates became my greatest accomplishment.

Now, as I prepare to join the physical therapy program at Pima, I am thrilled to embark on this new career path as a Physical Therapy Assistant (PTA). I am filled with excitement for this transition and eagerly await the commencement of this new journey. I look forward to meeting all of you and making a positive impact on the lives of my future patients, helping them improve their physical condition and achieve their goals in physical therapy.

rewrite my email:Dear hiring manager,

I hope this email finds you well, my name is <PRESIDIO_ANONYMIZED_PERSON>, and I hope to apply for Substitute teacher at PRLC preschool. I’ve been in early childhood education since graducation in 2016. From an assistant teacher to lead teacher to content creator, I’m always on my jouney to learn and excel  . After moving to Seattle, I worked in Ourbegining as a preschool teacher, then  I’ve decided to go back to school, and now I’m seeking a part time as a preschool teacher. You’ll find my resume and cover letter in my attachments, if you have any questions or additional materials that are needed, feel free to contact me.

Thank you!
Feiyan Guo

Dear Hiring Manager,

I hope this email finds you in good health. My name is <PRESIDIO_ANONYMIZED_PERSON>, and I am interested in applying for the position of Substitute Teacher at PRLC Preschool. I have been involved in the field of early childhood education since graduating in 2016. Throughout my journey, I have gained experience as an assistant teacher, a lead teacher, and even as a content creator. I am always eager to learn and strive for excellence.

Upon moving to Seattle, I worked at Ourbegining as a preschool teacher. However, I have made the decision to further my education by returning to school. At this time, I am actively seeking part-time opportunities as a preschool teacher. I have attached my resume and cover letter for your review. If you have any questions or require any additional materials, please feel free to contact me.

Thank you for considering my application.

Sincerely,
<PRESIDIO_ANONYMIZED_PERSON>
check my subject:application for substitute teacher

Your subject line, "Application for Substitute Teacher," is clear and concise. It effectively communicates the purpose of your email and lets the hiring manager know the main content of your message. Overall, it is a suitable subject line for your email.

is my email polite enough? Dear Hiring Manager,
 
 I hope this email finds you well. My name is <PRESIDIO_ANONYMIZED_PERSON>, and I am interested in applying for the position of Substitute Teacher at PRLC Preschool. I have been in early childhood education since graduating in 2016. Throughout my journey, I have gained experience as an assistant teacher, a lead teacher, and an educational content creator. I am always eager to learn and strive for excellence.

At this time, I am seeking part-time opportunities as a preschool teacher. I have attached my resume and cover letter for your review. If you have any questions or require any additional materials, please feel free to contact me.

Yes, your email is polite and professional in tone. You start with an appropriate greeting and express well wishes. Throughout the email, you maintain a respectful tone and provide relevant information about your experience and qualifications. You conclude with a courteous thank you. Overall, your email demonstrates politeness and professionalism.
